He belonged to one of the principal families of Judah, the Zarhites or the descendants of Zerah, and is called "the Hushathite," probably from the place of his birth. Sibbechaiï¿½s great exploit, which gave him a place among the mighty men of Davidï¿½s army, was his single combat with Saph or Sippai, tire Philistine giant, in the battle at, Gezer or Gob.
